Chapter Three
Two permissions.
One purpose.
Sjood needs two things from iOS to work properly. Both are required for the core features.
Screen Time
Required
Sjood uses Apple's FamilyControls to lock distracting apps the moment the adhan begins — and unlock them when you check in after prayer.
Tap below to grant Screen Time access
Notifications
Required
Gentle reminders 15 minutes before each adhan. A quiet nudge so you never miss a prayer time again.
Tap below to allow notifications
What happens next
iOS will show you two permission dialogs. Tap Allow on both — Sjood cannot lock apps or remind you without them.

Privacy Policy
Effective: May 2026 · Sjood by Akili
What we collect
Sjood stores only what you give us: your name, city, prayer preferences, check-in history, and streak data. Everything lives on your device. We do not have a server-side user database.
Screen Time & App Lock
App locking is powered by Apple's FamilyControls framework, running entirely on-device. Sjood never knows which apps you have installed or what you do in them — only when to lock and unlock.
AI-generated duas
When you request a personalised dua, anonymised context (spiritual score 1–5, mood category) is sent to Anthropic's Claude API. No name, location, or device identifier is included. Anthropic's own privacy policy governs their handling of API requests.
Location
Your approximate location is used only to calculate local prayer times. It is not stored beyond the session and is never sent to any server.
Analytics & advertising
None. We do not use third-party analytics, crash reporters, or advertising SDKs. Sjood contains no ads and no trackers.
Contact
Questions or deletion requests: privacy@sjood.app
Terms of Use
Effective: May 2026 · Sjood by Akili
Free trial & subscription
Your 7-day free trial begins at first launch. You will not be charged until the trial ends. Cancel any time via iOS Settings → Your Name → Subscriptions. Cancellation takes effect at the end of your billing period.
Pricing
Monthly: $4.99/mo · Yearly: $24.99/yr ($2.08/mo) · Lifetime: $59.99 one-time. Prices may vary by region. All billing is handled by Apple through the App Store.
Acceptable use
Sjood is a prayer focus tool. You may not reverse-engineer, modify, or circumvent the app lock system. The app is intended for personal, non-commercial use only.
Prayer times disclaimer
Prayer times are algorithmically calculated and may differ slightly from your local mosque. Always verify Jumu'ah and Eid times with a trusted local source.
Limitation of liability
Sjood is provided as-is. We are not liable for missed prayers, incorrect prayer times, or any damages arising from use of the app.
Contact
Support: support@sjood.app
